$description: Design tokens following the W3C Design Tokens Community Group (DTCG) format. Single source of truth for all SQLI brand tokens.

usage.$description: Prescriptive color usage rules for generating correct dark mode and mixed-theme UIs

colorPairing.$description: Every semantic color has a matching *-content counterpart. Always pair them for guaranteed contrast.
rules: Use --color-primary as background => use --color-primary-content for text/icons on it
Use --color-secondary as background => use --color-secondary-content for text/icons on it
Use --color-accent as background => use --color-accent-content for text/icons on it
Use --color-neutral as background => use --color-neutral-content for text/icons on it
Use --color-info/success/warning/error as background => use their -content counterpart for text
Use --color-base-100/200/300 as background => use --color-base-content for text
Never mix a color with the -content of a different semantic group

backgroundScale.$description: The base scale (100/200/300) provides layered depth. Use for nesting surfaces.
rules: base-100: page-level background (outermost container)
base-200: card, sidebar, or section background (one level in)
base-300: nested element within a card, active states, hover backgrounds
surface: elevated surface (modals, popovers, dropdowns) - slightly above base-100
panel-solid: opaque overlay panels (fixed sidebars, toolbars)
panel-translucent: translucent overlay panels (mobile navigation, floating panels)

darkMode.$description: Rules for generating a complete dark mode from the token system
rules: Apply data-theme='sqli-dark' on <html> or any container element - all CSS variables switch automatically
All semantic variables (--color-primary, --color-base-*, --color-border, etc.) are remapped; no code changes needed
Primary color switches from Cobalt to Sky for better legibility on dark backgrounds
Shadows use darker rgba values (higher opacity) to remain visible against dark surfaces
Border color lightens just enough to provide subtle separation without harshness
The base scale inverts: base-100 is darkest (Midnight), base-300 is lightest (still dark)

mixedThemeZones.$description: Rules for using dark zones inside a light layout (sidebar, footer, hero) or vice-versa
rules: Wrap the dark zone in a container with data-theme='sqli-dark' - all var() references inside resolve to dark values
Each zone is self-contained: text, buttons, links, borders all adapt correctly within the zone
Common patterns: dark sidebar with light main content, dark footer, dark hero section, dark code blocks
Transitions between zones: use --color-border of the outer theme for the dividing edge
Avoid mixing variables across zone boundaries - components inside a dark zone must only use dark-resolved variables
Overlay (--color-overlay) respects the zone: use it inside the zone for modals scoped to that section

examples.notes: Sidebar gets Midnight background, Cream text, Sky links. Main content stays in light theme.
Footer text uses Cream on Midnight. Links/CTAs use Sky. Logo uses light variant.
Hero background is Midnight, heading is Cream, CTA button uses Sky/Midnight.

rules: Do NOT hardcode hex values - always use var(--color-*) so themes work automatically
Do NOT use --color-base-content on a --color-primary background - use --color-primary-content instead
Do NOT mix light-theme variables inside a data-theme='sqli-dark' zone (they are already remapped)
Do NOT use opacity on brand colors to create tints - use the provided semantic scale instead
Do NOT use --color-base-100 for text - it is a background token. Use --color-base-content for text
Do NOT nest data-theme zones deeper than 2 levels - it becomes hard to maintain and debug

brand.$description: Core brand color primitives. These are the 6 canonical SQLI colors. Use semantic tokens (not brand primitives) in application code.

cream.$description: Warm white. Light mode page background (base-100). In dark mode, used for primary text (base-content).

midnight.$description: Deep navy. Dark mode page background (base-100). In light mode, used for primary text (base-content).

cobalt.$description: Vivid blue. Primary interaction color in light mode (buttons, links, focus rings). Too saturated for dark backgrounds - use Sky instead.

sky.$description: Soft blue. Primary interaction color in dark mode (buttons, links, focus rings). Lighter than Cobalt for legibility on Midnight backgrounds.

cobalt-hover.$description: Darker Cobalt for hover/active states in light mode. Pair with primary-content for text.

sky-hover.$description: Darker Sky for hover/active states in dark mode. Pair with primary-content for text.

semantic.$description: Theme-specific semantic tokens. These are the variables consumed in application code via var(--color-*). Each color has a -content counterpart for guaranteed contrast.

light.$description: sqli-light theme (default). Cream backgrounds, Midnight text, Cobalt accents.

base-100.$description: Page background (Cream). Pair with base-content for text.

base-200.$description: Card/section background. Slightly darker than base-100. Pair with base-content.

base-300.$description: Nested/active element background. Darkest of the base scale. Pair with base-content.

base-content.$description: Default text color (Midnight). Use on any base-* background.

primary.$description: Primary action surfaces (Cobalt): buttons, links, focus rings. Pair with primary-content for text on this background.

primary-content.$description: Text/icons on primary background. Near-white for contrast on Cobalt.

primary-hover.$description: Hover state for primary surfaces. Slightly darker Cobalt.

secondary.$description: Secondary surfaces (Midnight). Use for emphasis blocks, dark zones within light layout.

secondary-content.$description: Text on secondary background (Sky). Provides contrast on Midnight.

accent.$description: Accent highlights (darker Cobalt). Use sparingly for visual emphasis.

info.$description: Info state (Sky). Use for informational alerts, badges. Pair with info-content.

success.$description: Success state. Use for confirmation alerts, badges. Pair with success-content.

warning.$description: Warning state. Use for warning alerts, badges. Pair with warning-content.

error.$description: Error state. Use for error alerts, badges. Pair with error-content.

border.$description: Default border. Subtle separator between sections. In dark mode, auto-adjusts to a lighter shade on dark backgrounds.

surface.$description: Elevated surface (modals, popovers, dropdowns). Slightly above base-100.

overlay.$description: Semi-transparent overlay for modals and drawers. Uses Midnight at 50% opacity.

dark.$description: sqli-dark theme. Midnight backgrounds, Cream text, Sky accents. Applied via data-theme='sqli-dark' on any container.

base-100.$description: Page background (Midnight). Pair with base-content for text.

base-200.$description: Card/section background. Slightly lighter than base-100. Pair with base-content.

base-300.$description: Nested/active element background. Lightest of the dark base scale. Pair with base-content.

base-content.$description: Default text color (Cream). Use on any base-* background in dark mode.

primary.$description: Primary action surfaces (Sky): buttons, links, focus rings. Lighter blue than Cobalt for legibility on dark backgrounds.

primary-content.$description: Text/icons on primary background (Midnight). Dark text on Sky.

primary-hover.$description: Hover state for primary surfaces. Slightly darker Sky.

secondary.$description: Secondary surfaces (Cream). Inverted from light mode for emphasis blocks.

accent.$description: Accent highlights (lighter Sky). Use sparingly for visual emphasis on dark backgrounds.

neutral.$description: Neutral surfaces (dark gray). Pair with neutral-content.

success.$description: Success state. Slightly lighter than light mode for visibility on dark. Pair with success-content.

error.$description: Error state. Slightly lighter than light mode for visibility on dark. Pair with error-content.

border.$description: Default border. Subtle light edge on dark backgrounds. Just enough contrast without harshness.

surface.$description: Elevated surface (modals, popovers). Same as base-200 in dark mode.

overlay.$description: Semi-transparent overlay for modals and drawers. Uses pure black at 60% (higher than light mode for depth).

typography.$description: Typography tokens. Font family and weight are theme-independent (same in light and dark).
